summary: Calling set_tile_animation_speed resets scenes added via ScenesCollection

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], v4.5.dev1.official [97241ffea]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

It seems that when I call the `set_tile_animation_speed` function, any scenes in that TileMapLayer added via ScenesCollection will reset their position (and even reappear if they've already been deleted).

I've attached an MRP below, but in case it helps, here's the code I'm running:

```gdscript
var source_id: int = tile_map_layer.tile_set.get_source_id(1)
if tile_map_layer.tile_set.get_source(source_id) is TileSetAtlasSource:
	var tile_set_atlas_source: TileSetAtlasSource = tile_map_layer.tile_set.get_source(source_id)
	tile_set_atlas_source.set_tile_animation_speed(Vector2.ZERO, 1.0)
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0a8523ba-bbd1-498f-bfa4-5e5dee4a1d9f)

### Steps to reproduce

- add a TileMapLayer node.
- add a TileSet to that TileMapLayer that includes a ScenesCollectionSource and an AtlasSource.
- place a scene in the tile map via the scenes collection.
- add a script that calls `set_tile_animation_speed` on the atlas source.
- calling `set_tile_animation_speed` should reset the ScenesCollection scenes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19495532/mrp.zip)

summary: Crash to open a project using Forward+ with Vulkan

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable [49a5bc7b6], 4.4.1.rc1, 4.4.1.rc2, 4.5.dev1.
- **Not reproducible in**: 4.4.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.2.stable, 4.1.stable.

### System information

- Windows 10 (10.0.19045)
- NVIDIA - GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (27.21.14.5720)
- Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8750H CPU @ 2.20GHz, 2201 Mhz, 6 Core(s), 12 Threads

### Issue description

When creating a new project and trying to open it, the editor simply closes by itself, this only occurs when using the Vulkan driver. Here is the editor's verbose: 

[editor-verbose.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19481450/editor-verbose.txt)

The problem is not limited to editor, games on this version are also affected, in MRP (see at the end) it is possible to test the drivers and modes, here is the result:

Mode | Driver | Command | Works
--- | --- | --- | ---
Forward+ | Vulkan | `test.exe --rendering-driver vulkan` | ❌
Mobile | Vulkan | `test.exe --rendering-method mobile` | ❌
Forward+ | D3D12 | `test.exe --rendering-driver d3d12` | ✔️
Compatibility | opengl3 | `test.exe --rendering-driver opengl3` |✔️

Here is the GAME's verbose: 

[game-verbose.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19481538/game-verbose.txt)

I also removed/disabled the NVidia driver, and performed the tests with the "Intel(R) UHD Graphics 630", the same failures occurred, except that the **Mobile mode worked**.

### Steps to reproduce

Editor:
- Open the Godot editor
- Create a new project
- Then open the project
- The editor will close

Games:
- The game will only crash if you use Vulkan
- Using D3D12 will not have any problems, it will work normally.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Create a new project and open it.

To test the problem independently you can follow the minimal example and export to Windows:

[mcve-crash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19481521/mcve-crash.zip)

then run the commands:

```
test.exe --rendering-driver vulkan
test.exe --rendering-method mobile
test.exe --rendering-driver d3d12
test.exe --rendering-driver opengl3
```

summary: Add comments above Object binding macros, rework PropertyHint/PropertyUsage comments

description.text:
These comments are displayed when hovering the macros in many C++ editors.

The PropertyHint and PropertyUsage comments have been fully reworked to replicate the information contained in the class reference. While this results in duplicated documentation, this removes the need to cross-reference the class reference when using these types which are frequently used when binding new properties.

We can extend this later to other commonly used macros like `VARIANT_ENUM_CAST()`, `BIND_ENUM_CONSTANT()`, `GDVIRTUAL*` and so on.

- See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104582#issuecomment-2749783031.

## Preview

Visual Studio Code:

summary: [4.5 dev1] Borderless maximized window overlaps taskbar in Windows 10

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproduceable in 4.5 dev1
- Not reproduceable in 4.4 stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot 4.5 dev1

### Issue description

In Godot 4.4 stable (and AFAIK before)  maximizing a borderless window respected the taskbar in Windows 10 (at least in my tests) like expected for a maximized window.

4.5 dev1, to be precise db7c94bd655d2cb99c7522daf5f288ddafc9d814 silently handles borderless maximize as Fullscreen:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blame/master/platform/windows/display_server_windows.cpp#L2398

This leads to the window now hiding the taskbar, as is expected for a window in FULLSCREEN mode but not in MAXIMIZED mode.

This change somewhat "fixed" https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/99952 (not being able to maximize borderless windows on anythng than the first screen) although the result is obviously still wrong if the taskbar stretches over all monitors.

(With the new window_start_drag / window_start_resize i thought i could finally do my own custom titlebar, but it seems more work is required there)

### Steps to reproduce

(In code: Just set borderless = true followed by mode = MODE_MAXIMIZED on a window)

Run BorderlessDemo project, check "borderless" checkbox, click maximize.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[BorderlessDemo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19473699/BorderlessDemo.zip)

summary: Mouse and touch input fight for control when used simultaneously

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: every Godot 4 build (regardless of type) from 4.0 stable to the current 4.5.1. Currently happening for me in 4.4.1.stable .NET. and happened to me in 4.3.stable .NET.
- Also reproducible on other computers with other types of touch devices.

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot 4.4.1 .NET stable - [Lenovo L13 Yoga](https://www.lenovo.com/ca/en/p/laptops/thinkpad/thinkpadl/thinkpad-l13-yoga/22tpl13l3y1)

### Issue description

Using any sort of touch device or pen alongside a mouse causes a desync between the system cursor position and Godot's understanding thereof. What this means is that using touch input will often fire events at the system cursor position as well as/rather than where the touch is actually happening. In the in-built text editor, this results in the cursor jumping back to the system position immediately after the tap. My laptop moves the cursor position after the event fires, meaning it's always one touch behind, whereas the pen + tablet I tested with would simply always revert to where the system cursor was.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open a new project.
- Go to the script tab.
- Make a bunch of newlines.
- Move your mouse cursor somewhere down and click.
- Tap elsewhere in the text editor on a touch screen.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

I have a node with an attached `@tool` script which lets me connect a specific signal from that node to a specific method of a given target node via a setter method. When I do this, the signal is connected as expected, but the signal connection does not appear in the _Signals_ panel of the _Node_ dock. Only when I select a different node and then re-select the original node does the connection appear in the _Signals_ panel. Calling `notify_property_list_changed` from the script after connecting the signal _does not_ cause the connections in the _Signals_ panel to refresh.

6. Switch to the _Node_ dock and open the _Signals_ panel, if it is not already open.
    1. **Expected behavior:** The `my_signal` signal should show a connection to `../TargetNode :: print_tree()`.
    2. **Actual behavior:** No connection is shown under `my_signal`

summary: Double Precision Multi-mesh based rendering applies transforms incorrectly

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in all double precision builds up to v4.5.dev.custom_build [594d64ec2]
Single precision builds are unaffected.

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (594d64ec2) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

After many years(!) of battle various double precision issues and having failed to make any headway in the GLSL, I'm now moving my efforts to documentation in hopes that others can find the core issue.

It is my current conclusion that all four issues described here, some previously reported, are all caused by a order of operations bug in the conditional `#ifdef USE_DOUBLE_PRECISION` blocks used in `scene_forward_clustered.glsl`. I have tried hundreds of iterations but no combination has fixed an issue without causing other, sometimes worse, issues. 

# The Big Four

## Multimesh Rotation
A multimesh instance rotated 30 degrees on the z-axis improperly rotates the instances and then applies the position.

| Single Precision | Double Precision |
| ----------------- | ------------------- |
| ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5eefc2d1-7927-4a08-9d56-8f7ca19a10e8) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d562ed8d-bfd8-4830-b9ad-7acbaae122d7) | 

Relevant open issues or PRs: #101139, #76388, #89061
Previous PRs: #76003

## Particle Billboarding
Particles set to billboard will only work when their global rotation is zero. There is an open PR to fix but it breaks local_coord particles even more than they currently are.

| Single Precision | Double Precision |
| ----------------- | ------------------- |
| ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f7866b8-f1b5-402d-937f-f7056b5d5a3d) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b1159a4-5ddf-4304-92f1-5ded538c8eaa) |

Relevant open issues or PRs: #76388, #89165

## Particles with local coordinates ignore parent rotation
When using local coordinates, the particles behave strangle and continue to emit in the global direction. When using the fix for billboarding in #89165 they fly toward the camera.

| Single Precision | Double Precision |
| ----------------- | ------------------- |
| ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6880729-6153-4419-9bd4-8882bc2130db) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6147500b-0502-4ad6-89ea-a6b0c3b46b74) |

Relevant open issues or PRs: #73383, #89061

## Clip space outlines render at the origin on multimesh instances
When using a standard clip space outline shader, all of the outlines are rendered at the origin of the multimesh.

Here are a handful of other issues and PRs that may be relevant to the core issue as they deal with various `_MATRIX` methods in double precision:

## Prior work

I believe @ecmjohnson's work in #89165 is working in the right area but it's unclear why fixing some things makes everything worse.

I believe @fire and @clayjohn are the architects of the original system and may have high level ideas on why this is happening.

I have tried many iterations of moving, storing and applying the split transformation code but I admit many of those changes were random in hope of seeing progress. 

## Next steps

I'm desperately looking for someone to help fix this multi-mesh based issue for those of us in double precision rendering. At this point, it is becoming a blocker for our title. If you have the skill set, but not the time, please reach out.

summary: Exporting .png and Dynamic Loading with load()

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cb]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable

### Issue description

In the export settings, there is an option to "Export all resources in the project", but it doesn't work exactly as expected. As I understand it, it actually means "export everything in the project if Godot considers it used."

Essentially, everything that appears in the project tree is treated as a Resource, and most formats - such as .png - are definitely considered resources. If we place a .png file in the scene, it will be included in the exported project. Similarly, if we load it using load(), it will also be included.

However, when I use a dynamic path
```
func loadIcons(path: String) -> Dictionary:
	var result: Dictionary = {}
	var dir: DirAccess = DirAccess.open(path)
	if dir:
		dir.list_dir_begin()
		var fileName
		while fileName != "":
			fileName = dir.get_next()
			if fileName.ends_with(".png"):
				var key = fileName.get_basename()
				var texture = load(path + "/" + fileName)
				result[key] = texture
	return result
````
these files are missing in the export.

However, specifying *.png for forced export in the export settings doesn't help, because—if I understand correctly—it only applies to alternative file types. For example, I can use *.ktx2, and it will always be exported, but with .png, .webp, etc., this doesn't work.

Additionally, if we check what this function detects, we can see an import entry for the .png file, but the actual image will not be present in the exported project.

### Steps to reproduce

https://youtu.be/JRAAmS5Ftzo

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: 3D meshes causes game to crash on compatibility mode on android [PowerVR BXM-8-256]

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.1.rc2.official [abef5e0d2], v4.4.1.rc1.official [daa4b058e], v4.5.dev1.official [97241ffea]

Not reproducible in: v4.0.stable.official [92bee43ad], v4.1.stable.official [970459615]

### System information

Motorola moto g73 5G, android 14 U1TNS34.82-12-7-9, Android SDK 34, OpenJDK 24 (also tested with 17)

### Issue description

I first noted the issue when trying to export a basic 3D scene to android and running it on my phone. I noticed that every time I tried to open the game, it would show the Godot's logo and then close the game right after. 

Not only that, but I tried to install the mobile version of the engine and open my project directly on my phone instead of exporting it, but it would try to open the project and then close the app. My next attempt was to create a new project using the mobile engine and the compatibility renderer, but every time I tried to add a mesh to a MeshInstance3D node, the app would just close (probably why my other project didn't even open, I had a 3d mesh in the main scene). But when I switched to the mobile renderer, I didn't have any problem adding meshes and running the game.

I tried to use remote debug on my PC by connecting my phone via USB to test changing the renderer on my other project (because I couldn't open it on my phone to change it) and I got the same result, it works fine on the mobile renderer but not on the compatibility.

Another thing I tried is adding a mesh to the scene, but behind the camera, I noticed that when the camera is not looking at it everything works fine, but when the mesh enters the view the game closes.

Also, I don't get any warnings or errors, the game just closes.

I've tested the same project on Godot 4.0, 4.1, 4.3, 4.4, 4.4.1 RC 1 & 2, 4.5dev1. Except for 4.0 and 4.1, I got the same result in every other version, the game works on the mobile renderer and not on compatibility.

I will try to test more versions to find out exactly when this issue was introduced, but it takes time because I have to download a new export template for every version I test.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new project on any of the specified versions of Godot using the compatibility renderer. Add a Node3D as the parent node with a Camera3D and a MeshInstance3D with a BoxMesh as children. Point the camera at the mesh and try o open the game on an Android device using remote debug

summary: Expose TriangleMesh api functions wrapped for scripting
Project manager: Add option to backup project when it will be changed

description.text:
The goal is to allow users to easily backup their projects if Godot will make irreversible changes to them. While this could make it more likely that folks will make backups in general, I'm mainly thinking about Android where managing files at all can be difficult (or require installing additional apps to make it not difficult).

Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/2292

## Screenshots

### Opening an Old Project

If opening a project will lead to irreversible changes, it adds a "Backup project first" checkbox:

![Selection_317](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d0c2706b-fb71-4407-b0d6-e2b48d3eaa49)

If you check it and click "OK" (or "Full Conversion" if opening a Godot 3 project), then it'll take the user to a variation on the usual dialog for creating a project, in order to allow them to pick the directory and name the project:

![Selection_318](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a185ebf-e3d4-45e6-b6e8-ddaf5853acbf)

After clicking "Duplicate", it will duplicate the project, but then continue on to open the original project.

### Duplicating a project directly

This PR also adds a "Duplicate" button to the right sidebar:

![Selection_315](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/091f50ff-3f9a-40cf-8e1e-93ee343c9b03)

If the user selects a project, and then clicks this "Duplicate" button, it'll take the user to a variation on the usual dialog for creating a project similar to the one shown above:

![Selection_316](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/44b30bb9-f276-415b-9918-2057436b6839)

However, this time it includes the "Edit Now" checkbox, which if checked, will lead to the newly duplicated project to be opened. If not, it only does the duplication, and then closes this dialog, returning the user to the list of projects.

## Caveats

- There is no progress dialog when copying the project: it just freezes until the copy is finished. However, it does the same thing when extracting a project from a ZIP file. We probably should have a progress dialog here for both installing from ZIP and project duplication, but since this matches pre-existing behavior, it seems like something that can be handled in a future PR

## Original List of Alternative Approaches

**UPDATE (2025-04-07):** The current PR now implements alternatives nr 1 and 2. I'm just keeping this section here so that early comments referring to these approaches still make sense :-)

1. Rather than sneaking in a "Backup Project" button into the dialog when changes are possible, we could have a "Copy Project" project button on the side and it'd be up to the user to use that button on their own. This takes the user out of the flow, so they may be less likely to do it, but it would be less weird that the project doesn't get opened immediately after completing the backup. Mockup:
    ![Selection_307](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21b0e041-1b84-4faf-9429-70adebcb3720)
2. Rather than being a button, there could be a "Make a backup first" checkbox in the dialog. Then regardless of which button the user clicks, it'll go to the dialog to make the backup, and then afterwards proceed to opening the project. Mockup:
    ![Selection_308](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2cbf96d-b8ea-4793-88a3-2d21437a32be)
3. Rather than making a copy of the old project, and then opening the original (upgrading it), should this open the copy (leaving the original alone)? I was torn on which way to go, and I'm not sure I picked the right one. I guess if you think of the upgraded project as a continuation of the original, it's nice for that to be in the same directory going forward?

summary: Inconsistent Parse Error from script with cyclic dependency with static initialization

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4 stable, 4.5.dev1

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11

### Issue description

There is a parse error as a result of a circular reference during the static initialization of the script.
The script must have a cyclic dependency with a resource and be opened in the script tab to produce the error:
```
ERROR: scene/resources/resource_format_text.cpp:39 - res://some_resource.tres:6 - Parse Error: .
ERROR: Failed loading resource: res://some_resource.tres. Make sure resources have been imported by opening the project in the editor at least once.
```
The error can be produced by a dependency created by a preload in any script, or a load in a `@tool` script.

There does seem to be a situation in which the error always appear on first edit no matter what, and produced twice if the offending script uses preload, as mentioned in:
#99333

There are several ways in which the error seem to resolve:
1. If the offending script is not opened in the script tab
2. If the offending resource is opened before the offending script
3. If a different script references the offending resource class via static typing is opened before or when both are loaded together upon reload (i.e. both loaded in script tab)
   - it appears to work regardless of scope so long as it's in a different file even in the below code seem to fix the problem without ever instantiating the class or calling the function in the class:
```gdscript
class Inner:
	func inner_do() -> void:
		for i : SomeResource in []:
			pass
```
4. Same as 3, but instead of static type reference, using `preload`; however, now must always be loaded before the offending script 
   - When loaded in script tab together, the script's name must alphanumerically come before the offending script lest the error pop up
5. Same as 4, but instead of `preload`, you may use a `load`, but it must be a `@tool` and be initialized during static init
   - `load` is limited to initializing static fields or be in `_static_init()` to avoid error
6. If you extend script that is mentioned in 3-5 onto a scene and have the scene opened, upon reload, it will always load before the offending script

I would like there to be no errors  upon reload, otherwise have it consistently throw the error because when I discovered the error, I already made a system that contained no error using the cyclic reference, but when I chose to recreate it for a different class without a reference from another script, the error popped up.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Import MRP - Error
3. Reload - No Error
4. Open `some_resource.gd` - Error
5. Reload - Error
6. Close `some_resource.gd` and reload - No Error
7. Open `some_resource.tres` then open `some_resource.gd` - No Error
8. Open `script.gd` and uncomment the following:
```gdscript
class Inner:
	func something() -> void:
		for i in []:
			if i is SomeResource:
				pass
			#if i == preload("res://some_resource.tres"):
				#pass
```
   Then save and reload - No Error
9. Close `script.gd` and reload - Error

From here, you can use the MRP as a playground to test the specifics I've stated in the issue description by commenting and uncommenting different  lines in `script.gd`, you can even close `script.gd` and open `z-script.gd` which should be the same, but will behave differently due to `load` and `preload` on reolad as noted in item 4 and 5 in issue description. As well as use `script_scene.tscn` to see how having a scene tab that has `script.gd` works when `script.gd` itself is closed as per item 6 in issue description.

All instances, but initial edit, `some_resources.gd` must be opened to produce error.

This isn't 100% comprehensive, but it may be enough for the developers to diagnose the problem properly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[cyclic_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19440100/cyclic_mrp.zip)

summary: Change `.build` extension for engine build profiles to `.gdbuild`

description.text:
`.build` was already used by the Meson buildsystem for its build file. Using a unique file extension for Godot avoids ambiguity and allows third-party tools to reliably detect its file formats.

The previous file extension is still accepted in the load/save dialog, so this change is backwards-compatible. The buildsystem doesn't care about the file extension, so no changes are required on that end.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11816.

summary: Tweak ReflectionProbe roughness according to hit distance when box projection is used

description.text:
This improves the realism of reflections when box projection is enabled. This works by scaling the roughness according to the hit distance between the bounds of the reflection and the fragment being shaded.

Additionally, this makes reflection sampling in Compatibility use the same perceptual roughness formula (`sqrt(roughness)`) as Forward+/Mobile for better visual consistency.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11670.

**Testing project:** [test_improved_box_projected_reflections.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19439595/test_improved_box_projected_reflections.zip)
*Press <kbd>B</kbd> to toggle box projection.*

summary: Crash when trying to save tool scene

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.3.2) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 (Apple8) - Apple M2 (8 threads)

### Issue description

I'm working on a portals plugin, where a `@tool` script spawns other nodes while being set up. 

When I toggle `is_teleport` boolean to _true_ in the inspector, the script creates an `Area3D` node with a `CollisionShape3D` child. Both are stored in exported property fields.

When I set the `is_teleport` to _false_, I only call `queue_free` only on the area, assuming the collider will get cleaned up too (it's the area's child). But it does not - it remains visible in the inspector exported field. When I try to save, the editor crashes.



My guess would be that it tries to serialize a freed node. When I manually clear the collider property, it saves fine.

### Steps to reproduce

summary: Improving editor's auto-translation

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5 after 68d983395f4e127d20d6319c2404fa0ba6d7e523

### System information

W10

### Issue description

With #102562 merged, we can now change editor's language without restarting. However the option is still marked as requiring restart, because it's not complete. The editor needs to properly utilize Godot's auto-translation system to allow full language switching. There is lots of legacy code that prevents that, with the main culprit being TTR method that translates strings in place, thus preventing auto-translation to occur.

Here are some guidelines that should be used from now on, and applied to old code, to have proper auto-translation in the editor:
- For static translateable strings, i.e. Labels that are assigned once etc., TTRC should be used.
- For dynamic translateable strings, TTR should be used with combination of vformat (e.g. `vformat(TTR("%d times greater"), times)`).
  - If the strings are being re-created every time they display, e.g. in context menus that are usually cleared on each popup, nothing more needs to be done.
  - For strings that don't update every time they are shown, NOTIFIACTION_TRANSLATION_CHANGED should be used. I don't think we are doing that already somewhere, so this needs to be established.

You can use the above guidelines to find code that needs to be updated, if it doesn't follow them. Alternatively you can open editor, change language and look for strings that didn't change (best tested with translations that have high completion percentage, as some texts won't translate otherwise). Feel free to open pull requests referencing this issue.

I'm opening this issue as a pseudo-tracker. I don't know every area that needs changes, but the issue is going to be open until there is no more strings that need updating.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open editor with non-English language
2. Change language
3. Look for text that still uses previous language

summary: Compress textures with storage compression on import

description.text:
This performs a compression step for textures that have the VRAM Compressed, VRAM Uncompressed and Basis Universal compression modes.

This usually reduces file size for each compressed texture by a factor of 2, without affecting the memory taken by the texture after it's loaded. This can make for significantly smaller PCK sizes and therefore faster downloads.

Additionally, for image pixel formats that cannot be VRAM-compressed (such as HDR + alpha), this allows having some form of storage compression to reduce file size by a potentially much larger factor.

This can be disabled in the Project Settings if having the fastest possible import/load times is desired, at the cost of larger PCK sizes.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11852.

**Testing project:** [test_vram_compress_lossless.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19438036/test_vram_compress_lossless.zip)

Non-embedded Borderless windows glitches when changing to any mode other than Windowed

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6109) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900KF CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When setting the borderless property of a window node, changing it's mode to any mode other than Windowed will cause the window to enter in a glitchy state.
- It will become unusable and unclickable.
- It will also vanish from the system window management, although it's possible to recover it's visibility by making it invisible and visible again (via the visibility property or via the show/hide functions)

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the Project Settings
- Set display/window/subwindows/embed_subwindows to false
- Create a Window Node
  - Set borderless to true
- Run the game (debug or release build)
- Change the window mode to a mode different than "Window" in any way (via script, keyboard shortcuts like Win+Up and etc)
